title: Ensure HyperV 9p mounts work when a dir doesn't exist
body:
Before, we required that the mount target exist and be a directory for the 9p mount to successfully complete, which is not how things are supposed to work - the user should be able to mount anywhere. This should just be a simple mkdir, but with FCOS the root directory is immutable so we need to undo that before we can mkdir, and unfortunately we don't have a library that can do chattr (and I didn't want to drag in a new dependency just for that), so let's be gross and add it to the SSH command. I aggressively dislike this but it does work.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] Can worry about getting a more generic mount test together for Machine later.

Are the Windows tests even running? I don't see them in the list of completed tests?

I don't think so but that doesn't really prevent us from having a test added that will be run later.
The fine difference between path.Clean() and filepath.Clean() here is not something most people notice and thus might change and break at the next rework.
I will not block over it here as I assume we should add some tests once we vendor in the default hyperV volumes from c/common as this will be important for it.

Changes LGTM
I too would like to see a test at some point.
Sqlite test is failing, I'm not sure if that's expected or another rerun should be done.
@mheon want to add a test and get this across the finish line?
Sure

seeing
```
  > Enter [It] podman volume on non-standard path - C:/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/cirrus-ci-build/repo/pkg/machine/e2e/basic_test.go:99 @ 02/21/24 22:01:12.29

      <int>: 125
  to match exit code:
      <int>: 0
```

so something wrong with the vsock?
Cockpit tests failed for commit 49039a0cde5586da126f600b0467ac009f619817. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

still seeing `Error: dialing vsock port 5377136: dial vsock host(2):5377136: connect: connection timed out` in your test.
This seem to be a race condition regarding your hyperV mount code.

For some reason hyperV is not using `MountVolumesToVM` and is doing this before the ready check?
Also there is no ready check at all for the server process on windows so this is always race unless you actually check that the server process is listening before we launch the client process in the VM.
Or maybe easier build some retry mechanism in the VM client process to retry a couple of time before giving up.

The refactor broke it, looks like; used to start at the same time as gvproxy, now it's immediately prior to SSHing into the VM.
Unfortunately we don't get a "ready" signal from the 9p library that the server is active and accepting connections so I don't know how easily this can be fixed.
The test will likely need to be skipped on WSL as well. Do we have macros for that already?

> The refactor broke it, looks like; used to start at the same time as gvproxy, now it's immediately prior to SSHing into the VM. Unfortunately we don't get a "ready" signal from the 9p library that the server is active and accepting connections so I don't know how easily this can be fixed.

Lets just go with the retry in the client then. The ssh itself is blocking so it will not return until it mounted all the shares.

> The test will likely need to be skipped on WSL as well. Do we have macros for that already?

skipIfWSL()?
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit f272ebc58822d3754d6fb0eba36d2a61eacfb443. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
1.5 seconds was not a sufficient backoff evidently.
Changed to 5 seconds
machine-hyperv-windows test failed. I won't restart, because I don't want to mask real errors
The test is still failing the same way, I am not so sure if the issue is the timeout after all.
There is the same problem with the server9p command like we have with gvproxy, we start it without checking anything so if this command fails for whatever reason we do not know
Let me see if there's a deeper problem in the mount code from the refactor
OK, think I got it. We were listening on the wrong vsock number - missing * on a pointer, so we used pointer address, not value.
OK, just a silly slash vs backslash error left now
/lgtm

Eugh. Quay is having issues.
```
# Error: checking image updates for container cbee701dfa3363455653b73904c54ab56b720fb9c15621c232952749af60e87d: reading manifest latest in quay.io/libpod/alpine: received unexpected HTTP status: 504 Gateway Time-out
```
/hold cancel
